pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 9-17: ASSETS_DIR="/home/ga/assets" and AUDIO_FILE="$ASSETS_DIR/beat.wav"; Audio generated via Python wave module at line 32
setup_task.sh line 73: obj.name = "SpeakerCone"; export_result.sh line 49: bpy.data.objects.get("SpeakerCone")
export_result.sh line 60: fcurve.data_path == "scale" is the target F-Curve channel to be verified
export_result.sh line 60: fcurve.array_index == 2; In Blender Scale property, array indices are [0]=X, [1]=Y, [2]=Z, so 2 is Z-axis

verifier.py line 69: if key_count > 50: score += 30 (strict inequality, not >=). Metadata claims minimum is 50, but verifier requires strictly > 50, meaning 51 or more keyframes to earn criterion points.

verifier.py line 80: if variance > 0.1: score += 20 (strict inequality, not >=). Metadata claims minimum is 0.1, but verifier requires strictly > 0.1, meaning variance must exceed 0.1 to earn criterion points.

privileged_info_summary: The audio_reactive_visualizer task uses synthetically generated audio data (a 4-second, 44100 Hz WAV file with 100 Hz sine wave and linear decay envelope) and a programmatically created Blender scene containing a cylinder named SpeakerCone. The task requires baking this audio to the Z-axis (array_index 2) of the Scale property (data_path "scale") of the SpeakerCone object. Key contradiction found: metadata specifies min_keyframe_count as 50 and min_amplitude_variance as 0.1, but the verifier uses strict inequalities (> 50 and > 0.1 respectively), requiring 51+ keyframes and variance strictly exceeding 0.1 for criterion points.
